scenario: Unverified or malicious IP is accepted as trusted design logic.
evidence_requirement: Provider identity; package digest; signed release; IP intake review
selection_rationale: Retained: directly tied to IP artifact identity and external provider/repository intake boundary.
attacker_capability: Present an IP package under a misleading provider identity or approval status.
fpga_specific_rationale: Third-party IP becomes synthesizable hardware logic and propagates into downstream tool artifacts.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: HDL/IP is changed before synthesis while preserving expected project structure.
evidence_requirement: Source digest; review record; dependency/IP metadata manifest
selection_rationale: Retained: HDL/IP source integrity is an explicit transformation-input boundary.
attacker_capability: Modify HDL, packaged IP, or IP metadata before synthesis.
fpga_specific_rationale: Source and packaged IP encode design intent before transformation.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: HDL/IP leaks through repository, artifact storage, or build infrastructure.
evidence_requirement: Access log; repository policy; export record
selection_rationale: Retained: confidentiality applies to proprietary HDL/IP artifacts across repository, storage, and export boundaries.
attacker_capability: Read or export proprietary HDL/IP from repositories, build storage, or release bundles.
fpga_specific_rationale: HDL/IP exposes design value and reusable hardware structure.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Verification collateral is changed or cannot be tied to reviewed design evidence.
evidence_requirement: Testbench digest; simulation log; expected-result manifest
selection_rationale: Retained: simulation collateral is documented development evidence and has a distinct design-model-to-evidence boundary.
attacker_capability: Modify testbenches, golden vectors, simulation models, scripts, logs, or simulation reports.
fpga_specific_rationale: Simulation artifacts are development evidence before implementation and release.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Waveforms, result logs, coverage records, VCD/WDB/SAIF-style files, or simulator-written evidence are exposed, altered, or omitted outside the reviewed verification flow.
evidence_requirement: Result manifest; waveform/log digest; simulation command; access log
selection_rationale: Retained after DFD coverage check: simulation result evidence is documented by vendor flows and has evidence requirements distinct from testbench/source collateral.
attacker_capability: Read, alter, omit, or relabel simulation-result files, waveform records, simulator logs, or coverage/result evidence.
fpga_specific_rationale: Simulation outputs can reveal internal hardware behavior and can support or undermine verification evidence before release.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by audited simulation artifact evidence and distinct evidence-requirement fields

artifact: Timing, pin, placement, or exception constraints
scenario: Constraints are altered without corresponding reviewed design intent.
evidence_requirement: Constraint digest; change rationale; reviewer approval; exception/waiver record
selection_rationale: Retained: constraint artifacts have FPGA-specific signoff meaning and can affect physical realization without HDL changes.
attacker_capability: Alter XDC/SDC/PDC/FDC/NDC constraints, timing exceptions, pin constraints, or waivers without review evidence.
fpga_specific_rationale: Constraints affect physical realization and timing closure without changing HDL.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Tool options or scripts alter optimization, hierarchy, seed, security settings, or generated artifacts.
evidence_requirement: Command manifest; option snapshot; tool version; security-option record
selection_rationale: Retained: scripts, options, seeds, hierarchy controls, and security options shape generated FPGA artifacts.
attacker_capability: Alter scripts, tool options, seeds, hierarchy controls, security options, or implementation settings.
fpga_specific_rationale: Tool configuration shapes netlists, reports, bitstream identity, and programming outputs.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Constraint or configuration changes cause repeated closure or resource failures.
evidence_requirement: Constraint history; build-resource log; failure record
selection_rationale: Retained: availability is tied to FPGA timing, routing, and resource closure rather than only host service uptime.
attacker_capability: Introduce constraints/settings that cause repeated timing, routing, or resource failures.
fpga_specific_rationale: FPGA availability can fail through timing/resource closure, not only host downtime.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

artifact: Tool binary, plugin, Tcl package, or build container
trust_boundary: Toolchain environment to artifact transformation run
scenario: A substituted tool environment produces netlists, reports, or bitstreams that appear to come from the approved flow.
evidence_requirement: Tool/image digest; installation manifest; plugin/package digest; runner provenance; tool-version attestation
selection_rationale: Retained after vendor-corpus refresh: tool binary, plugin, Tcl package, IP generator, or build-container identity forms a distinct transformation-environment boundary.
attacker_capability: Substitute or alter the EDA executable, plugin, Tcl package, IP generator, or build container used to produce artifacts.
fpga_specific_rationale: EDA tools are representation transformers; environment identity can affect hardware artifacts even when HDL and constraints are unchanged.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

trust_boundary: License source/state to tool or IP availability
scenario: A build or IP-generation step depends on an undocumented or ambiguous license source or license state, making build authorization or availability evidence unclear.
evidence_requirement: License file/server record; license report; feature log; build log
selection_rationale: Retained after DFD coverage check: license files, license servers, license utilities, and license reports are documented toolchain dependencies, but the record is phrased as provenance/availability evidence rather than exploitability.
attacker_capability: Change, hide, omit, or fail to record license-source or license-state evidence for a tool or IP-dependent build step.
fpga_specific_rationale: License state can affect whether documented tools or IP-generating steps are available and whether a build can be explained later.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by audited license-dependency evidence; not a vendor vulnerability claim

trust_boundary: Tool/IP version state to generated artifact lineage
scenario: Artifacts are generated with an unexpected tool version, interim update, IP upgrade state, or version-compatible database state not bound to provenance.
evidence_requirement: Tool version; IP status or upgrade log; installation manifest; database-version record
selection_rationale: Retained after DFD coverage check: vendor documentation records tool versions, IP status/upgrade flows, interim-update or version-compatible database concepts that can affect artifact lineage.
attacker_capability: Change, downgrade, upgrade, or omit the tool/IP version state used to generate FPGA artifacts.
fpga_specific_rationale: FPGA artifacts may depend on tool, IP-generator, and database-version state even when source and constraints are unchanged.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by audited version/update provenance evidence

scenario: A generated netlist or synthesis checkpoint is modified or substituted.
evidence_requirement: Input-output digest chain; synthesis log
selection_rationale: Retained: generated representations are documented transformation artifacts requiring lineage and integrity evidence.
attacker_capability: Modify or substitute generated netlists, synthesized design checkpoints, or equivalent intermediate outputs.
fpga_specific_rationale: Intermediate hardware representations may be less reviewable than source but determine downstream implementation.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Implementation checkpoints, seeds, or route databases are manipulated or stale state is reused.
evidence_requirement: Seed/options manifest; checkpoint digest; route lineage
selection_rationale: Retained: implementation state has distinct lineage evidence across synthesis output, place-and-route state, and generated programming artifacts.
attacker_capability: Reuse stale implementation state or alter checkpoints, seeds, route databases, or option history.
fpga_specific_rationale: Implementation state binds design intent to a concrete placed/routed realization.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Implementation is delayed or blocked through resource exhaustion or pathological constraints.
evidence_requirement: Build-resource log; options history; route failure record
selection_rationale: Retained: implementation availability can be affected through resource exhaustion, routing failure, or pathological constraints/settings.
attacker_capability: Drive implementation into resource exhaustion, routing failure, or pathological runtime through constraints/settings.
fpga_specific_rationale: FPGA build availability depends on implementation feasibility and routing resources.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

trust_boundary: Tool output to human signoff/release decision
scenario: Signoff reports are modified, suppressed, or replaced.
evidence_requirement: Report digest; generator command; timestamp; exception/waiver record
selection_rationale: Retained: reports are documented engineering signoff evidence and can be altered independently from source artifacts.
attacker_capability: Modify, suppress, regenerate, or replace timing, utilization, DRC, power, exception, or signoff reports.
fpga_specific_rationale: Reports are engineering evidence for hardware configuration release decisions.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: A bitstream or report cannot be traced to source, constraints, options, tool identity, tool version, and approval.
evidence_requirement: Source-to-bitstream digest chain; CI identity; tool/image identity; attestation
selection_rationale: Retained: build lineage is central to artifact-provenance STRIDE and must bind source, constraints, tool identity, reports, and approval.
attacker_capability: Trigger or publish builds without durable linkage among source, constraints, options, tool identity, reports, and approval.
fpga_specific_rationale: Artifact meaning depends on lineage across multiple tool transformations.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Reports expose design structure, timing paths, resource use, IP composition, or constraints.
evidence_requirement: Report permissions; access logs; release classification
selection_rationale: Retained: reports/logs can disclose implementation structure, timing paths, resource use, IP composition, or constraints.
attacker_capability: Read reports/logs from build storage, report archives, or release bundles beyond intended recipients.
fpga_specific_rationale: Reports reveal hardware architecture and implementation constraints.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: A bitstream is substituted, mislabeled, stale, or replayed before release or programming.
evidence_requirement: Bitstream digest; signature where configured; release manifest; programming log; rollback record
selection_rationale: Retained: bitstream/programming files are deployable hardware-configuration artifacts with identity and substitution risk.
attacker_capability: Replace, relabel, replay, downgrade, or substitute a bitstream/programming file before release or programming.
fpga_specific_rationale: A bitstream/programming file is a deployable hardware configuration, not an arbitrary binary.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: Bitstreams, signing material, encryption material, or debug metadata leak through storage or release bundles.
evidence_requirement: Key-handling record; access log; encryption status; debug metadata classification
selection_rationale: Retained: confidentiality applies to bitstreams, signing/encryption materials, debug metadata, and release bundles.
attacker_capability: Read bitstreams, signing material, encryption material, debug metadata, or release bundles.
fpga_specific_rationale: Disclosure can expose design value or weaken configuration-control assumptions.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: An actor signs, releases, or promotes a bitstream without release authority.
evidence_requirement: Signer identity; approval record; release manifest
selection_rationale: Retained and split from programming-target authority: signing/release authorization uses distinct evidence requirements.
attacker_capability: Use signing, approval, promotion, or release authority outside the intended FPGA artifact workflow.
fpga_specific_rationale: Release/signing authority can authorize hardware configuration artifacts.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: A bitstream is programmed to an unauthorized or spoofed target, or programming occurs without deployment authority.
evidence_requirement: Target identity; programmer identity; programming log; rollback record
selection_rationale: Retained and split from signing/release authority: programming-target identity and deployment logs are distinct evidence requirements.
attacker_capability: Program an unauthorized target, spoof target identity, or alter/delete programming records.
fpga_specific_rationale: Programming binds a released artifact to an FPGA target and creates a deployment boundary.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

trust_boundary: Programming/debug evidence to deployment audit boundary
scenario: Programming status, readback or verify data, or debug-capture evidence is altered, omitted, or exposed outside the release policy.
evidence_requirement: Readback/status log; verify command; debug capture digest; access log
selection_rationale: Retained after DFD coverage check: programming status, readback/verify, programmer jobs, and debug-capture artifacts are documented, but runtime physical behavior remains out of scope.
attacker_capability: Alter, delete, omit, or disclose programming-status, readback/verify, or debug-capture evidence.
fpga_specific_rationale: Programming/readback evidence can support deployment accountability and may disclose implementation-sensitive state.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by audited programming/debug evidence and scope-limited to toolchain artifacts

scenario: Released artifacts lack durable provenance linking source, configuration, tool identity, reports, bitstream, and approval.
evidence_requirement: Workflow identity; artifact digest set; tool identity; attestation
selection_rationale: Retained: release provenance is explicit for FPGA artifact bundles crossing build/release boundaries.
attacker_capability: Release artifacts without complete manifests, workflow identity, digest sets, or approval records.
fpga_specific_rationale: FPGA artifacts require lineage across source/IP, constraints, reports, tool identity, bitstream identity, and release approval.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields

scenario: A release-automation actor releases or signs FPGA artifacts outside the authorized workflow.
evidence_requirement: Release approval; signing event; service account identity; protected-environment record
selection_rationale: Retained: release-automation authority can publish, sign, overwrite, or route hardware-configuration artifacts toward deployment.
attacker_capability: Abuse release-automation service accounts, runners, tokens, protected environments, or release jobs affecting FPGA artifacts.
fpga_specific_rationale: Release-automation authority can publish, sign, overwrite, or route hardware configuration artifacts toward deployment.
evidence_status: retained threat-model synthesis grounded by artifact, boundary, and evidence-requirement fields
